description:
cloudstack_private_gateway

Creates a private gateway for the given VPC.

NOTE: private gateway can only be created using a ROOT account!
example_usage:
resource "cloudstack_private_gateway" "default" {
  gateway    = "10.0.0.1"
  ip_address = "10.0.0.2"
  netmask    = "255.255.255.252"
  vlan       = "200"
  vpc_id     = "76f6e8dc-07e3-4971-b2a2-8831b0cc4cb4"
}

argument_reference.name: gateway
argument_reference.description: the gateway of the Private gateway. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: ip_address
argument_reference.description: the IP address of the Private gateway. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: netmask
argument_reference.description: The netmask of the Private gateway. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: vlan
argument_reference.description: The VLAN number (1-4095) the network will use.

argument_reference.name: physical_network_id
argument_reference.description: The ID of the physical network this private gateway belongs to.

argument_reference.name: network_offering
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the network offering to use for the private gateways network connection.

argument_reference.name: acl_id
argument_reference.description: The ACL ID that should be attached to the network.

argument_reference.name: vpc_id
argument_reference.description: The VPC ID in which to create this Private gateway. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

attributes_reference.name: id
attributes_reference.description: The ID of the private gateway.

import:
Private gateways can be imported; use <PRIVATE GATEWAY ID> as the import ID. For example:

terraform import cloudstack_private_gateway.default e42a24d2-46cb-4b18-9d41-382582fad309
